raw_consumer_statement:
I have formally dispute and demand resolution of charges on my Barclays account related to a purchase I made through XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX for airline tickets scheduled for travel on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX the time of booking, I opted into the travel insurance coverage offered by XXXX. 
However, when my transaction was processed, the insurance coverage I selected was not applied to the booking even though I was billed for it. I was not informed of any error until I contacted Priceline to make necessary adjustments to my return flight while on the trip. I received a conformation for my trip XX/XX/XXXX As a result, when my daughter became unwell and was unable to board the scheduled return flight, I was forced to purchase an additional flight on JetBlue to bring her home when the time came as opposed to being able to switch both flights. Had the insurance I selected at checkout been properly applied, these costs and adjustments would have been covered under the policy. Furthermore, cancelling the initial purchase was not an option, because the refund process would not allow the funds to be available to me immediately. Priceline 's failure to apply the insurance deprived me of both the coverage I selected and the ability to make an informed decision about continuing with the transaction. 
In addition, I was improperly pressured and coerced into opening a new Barclays credit card in connection with this purchase. This coercion constitutes a violation of the Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ) and the Consumer Financial Protection Act ( CFPA ), as it represents an unfair, deceptive, and abusive practice designed to manipulate my decision-making. Such conduct further invalidates the charges in question and reinforces the need for immediate corrective action. This transaction constitutes a clear violation of federal consumer protection laws and a breach of contract, as detailed below. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, at XXXX, I called Priceline as instructed by Barclay bank . I was informed by a supervisor that the insurance that I opted into was not applied because the bank failed to authorize the payment for the insurance. I was also told that the protection purchase failed due to either a transaction issue or a system error. However, I was billed for the insurance and the money was refunded to my account without my authorization, cancellation, notification. I was coerced into opening a credit card with Priceline with the expectation that I was purchasing a trip with insurance. I did not receive what was offered. I did not enter any card information because I opted into opening a line of credit.
